Infineon MCU Distributor - AURIX TC3xx, XMC4000, PSoC 6 Deep Stock

Infineon microcontrollers span a comprehensive portfolio designed for the most demanding embedded applications in automotive, industrial, and IoT markets. The AURIX(TM) TC3xx family leads the way in automotive-grade safety computing, integrating up to six TriCore(TM) cores with lockstep redundancy and hardware security modules certified to ISO 26262 ASIL-D. These devices power advanced driver-assistance systems, domain controllers, and electrification platforms where determinism and functional safety are non-negotiable. For industrial automation and motor drives, the XMC(TM) 4000 series combines an ARM(R) Cortex-M4F core with a rich set of peripherals including the Capture Compare Unit 4 (CCU4/CCU8) and the Position Interface (POSIF), enabling precision motor control without external signal processing hardware. The XMC1000 and XMC4000 lines are popular among engineers designing servo drives, PLC controllers, and energy-metering modules. IoT and edge applications benefit from the PSoC(TM) 6 dual-core architecture, pairing a Cortex-M4 application core with a Cortex-M0+ for ultra-low-power peripheral management. With BLE 5.0 connectivity and a programmable analog front-end, PSoC 6 enables sophisticated wearable devices, industrial sensors, and smart-building nodes. As an authorized BeiLuo Infineon MCU distributor, we maintain substantial inventory across all major series and package variants. Our FAE team provides hands-on evaluation board support, migration guidance between MCU families, and application-specific code review. Whether you are scaling production or prototyping a new platform, BeiLuo delivers verified Infineon MCUs with full traceability and competitive lead times.
Download Category Selection Guide Selection Guide →

FAE Technical Support

As a BeiLuo FAE with fifteen years in embedded design, I consistently recommend the AURIX TC3xx line when customers face ISO 26262 constraints - the on-chip lockstep and HSM save significant board-level BOM cost compared to discrete safety supervisors. For cost-sensitive industrial drives, the XMC4700 hits a sweet spot: 144 MHz Cortex-M4F, 2 MB flash, and hardware floating-point all on a 100-pin package. We stock both devices in volume and can typically ship within three business days. Our pre-sales review helps customers nail the right flash and RAM headroom on the first spin, avoiding costly re-spins.

Get FAE Consultation

MCU at a Glance

Key Parameters

  • Part No.
  • Series
  • Core Architecture
  • Flash (KB)
  • RAM (KB)
  • Max Freq (MHz)
  • Package
  • Stock

Typical Models

  • TC264DA — Dual-core 200 MHz AURIX TriCore MCU with 4 MB flash, ASIL-D hardware safety, and six CAN-FD nodes for automotive control.
  • XMC4700-F100K2048 — 144 MHz ARM Cortex-M4F MCU with 2 MB flash, 12-channel PWM, Ethernet MAC, and USB for industrial drives and PLCs.

Applications

  • Automotive Powertrain Control
  • Chassis & Safety Systems
  • EV Battery Management
  • Domain Controller Gateway
  • Industrial Motor Control
  • Industrial Servo Drives

Why BeiLuo

BeiLuo stocks Infineon AURIX and XMC MCUs with full lot traceability and FAE-backed functional-safety design support, not just parts, but qualified automotive and industrial firmware guidance.

Available MCU Models

Part No. Series Core Architecture Flash (KB) RAM (KB) Max Freq (MHz) Package Stock Action
TC264DA AURIX TC2xx TriCore 1.6P 4000 472 200 LQFP-176 inStock Details
XMC4700-F100K2048 XMC4700 ARM Cortex-M4F 2048 352 144 LQFP-100 inStock Details
